Details: Removal of the acetochlor degradate 2-ethyl-6-methylaniline by biological treatment was found to be quite effective in a bench-scale study. A mixed microbial culture primarily comprised of strains belonging to Kocuria and Bacillus was able to remove 99 percent of the contaminant. Over a nine day study of the biotransformation of the parent compound acetochlor, the concentration of 2-ethyl-6-methylaniline increased steadily until day four, where it was almost completely removed in 24 hours by the mixed culture.
